text: The 13th edition of the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 has just started. The search has begun and until October 1, people can be nominated who will make a major impact in making the built environment more sustainable. The spotlight is on individual efforts that make a collective difference. Nominations for the ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 awards are open. The ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 is the list of professionals who demonstrably contribute to a more sustainable built environment.

metadata.source_title: Nominations for ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 Awards have started

text:
Who are those people who have the decisiveness, charisma, contacts, knowledge and vision to really make the built environment more sustainable? There are three categories in which nominations can be made:. 1. The Creator: makes policy, designs, develops, advises and guides.
2. The Maker: produces and delivers sustainable concepts and products.
3. De Doener: builds, installs and assembles with an eye for circularity, energy efficiency and future value.

metadata.source_title: Nominations for ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 Awards have started

text: The overall winner of the Sustainable 50 comes from one of the above categories and wins both parts. In addition, the Young Professional Award will be presented again this year. From nominating to voting. Led by chairman Andy van den Dobbelsteen, professor of Climate Design & Sustainability and overall winner in 2024, the 12-member professional jury will assess the nominees. The professional jury awards points to each nominee based on [4 criteria](https://www.duurzaamgebouwd.nl/duurzaam-50).

metadata.source_title: Nominations for ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 Awards have started

text: In addition, the Young Professional Award will be presented again this year. From nominating to voting. Led by chairman Andy van den Dobbelsteen, professor of Climate Design & Sustainability and overall winner in 2024, the 12-member professional jury will assess the nominees. The professional jury awards points to each nominee based on [4 criteria](https://www.duurzaamgebouwd.nl/duurzaam-50). The jury then compiles a top 50 ranking and the public election will open from October 17.

metadata.source_title: Nominations for ABN AMRO Sustainable 50 Awards have started

text: From nominating to voting. Led by chairman Andy van den Dobbelsteen, professor of Climate Design & Sustainability and overall winner in 2024, the 12-member professional jury will assess the nominees. The professional jury awards points to each nominee based on [4 criteria](https://www.duurzaamgebouwd.nl/duurzaam-50). The jury then compiles a top 50 ranking and the public election will open from October 17. The awards will be presented at the [Climate Summit GO](http://www.climatetopgo.nl/) where all frontrunners will be welcomed at a festive ceremony on November 5, 2025.

Examples are the nitrogen dossier, the significant price increases for energy and building materials and also the personnel shortages. Driven by trends, innovations and legislation and regulations, the relevance of themes such as sustainability and environmental impact is growing and the sector is looking for a new balance.
